I'll start reaching out to UrbanNest and Dr. Mehta today.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_fatimah_alrashidi", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C006", "created_at": "2026-04-21T20:58:16+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_d86f3880_fatimah_creates_linear_4", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "6eba5a116ccaa8e34dc99bb9342bea59", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776793800.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "6ad4b276529dd70d0bb2cf7fb75743ac", "ts": "1776805620.000000", "text": "Chloe, I'm sorry — I don't have the signed placarding certificate on file. I need to be upfront about this.

When we did the Keiko Tanaka pickup in Columbus on April 15, I spoke with the Swift driver on the phone and he verbally confirmed that the Class 3B placards were affixed to the truck per the laser equipment handling requirements. He also said they'd completed the liability waiver that Mosaic's guidelines required. At the time I took that as confirmation and noted the move as on track.

But I never followed up to get the actual signed document. I meant to circle back with Swift's dispatch to request a copy of the signed cert and the waiver, but that same week I was also coordinating Tyler Okonkwo's Canopy Health move (his apartment handoff date kept shifting and the movers needed to reschedule twice) and the Zara Okoye prototype transport for Mosaic was happening simultaneously — that was the robotic arm shipment that ended up with the damage claim. I was juggling all three at once and the Tanaka paperwork just fell through the cracks.

I know that's not an excuse. The verbal confirmation doesn't count as documentation for DOT purposes — I should have gotten the signed cert before marking the move as dispatched.

I can reach out to Greg at Swift today and ask if they can pull the signed placarding cert from their records. If the driver did complete the placards at time of transport like he told me, they should have the documentation on their end. Want me to go ahead and contact them, or would you rather handle it directly given the DOT situation?

Again, I'm really sorry about this. It won't happen again.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_blessing_okafor", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C006", "created_at": "2026-04-21T21:07:00+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_1d400ba1_blessing_admits_gap_0", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "e1bb01f3f91314333595758257a2a588", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776804120.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "dcb194451a678906bf612befc0c51a49", "ts": "1776823140.000000", "text": "Marcus — the accrual is booked. Here are the details:

I've created a new liability account in QuickBooks: *Regulatory Compliance — DOT Penalties (Pending)*. This is classified as an Other Current Liability and sits under our contingent liabilities.

Accrual entry: *$50,000* as a contingent liability, booked today (April 21). The corresponding expense hits a new *Regulatory Compliance Expense* account. I've set the due date to June 5 to align with the extended DOT response deadline Marcus is requesting.

Document reference: ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, linked to a vendor record I created for U.S. DOT — PHMSA.

For context on the range of outcomes:
- Best case ($0): All documentation is completed and submitted successfully, DOT finds no violations, and we reverse the full accrual.
- Base case ($50K): DOT identifies the one confirmed gap (missing placarding cert for the Keiko Tanaka move) as a single violation. $50K represents the minimum expected penalty for one count under 49 CFR 107.329.
- Worst case ($200K+): DOT identifies multiple documentation deficiencies across the 4 flagged shipments, treating each as a separate violation. This scenario seems unlikely given Chloe's audit shows 3 of 4 moves have complete docs, but I wanted to flag the tail risk.

I booked the $50K as a reasonable estimate per ASC 450-20 since we have one confirmed documentation gap where loss is reasonably possible. We can adjust up or down once the audit resolves.

This will impact Q2 financial statements. I'll include a disclosure note in the Q2 close package referencing the DOT inquiry and the contingent liability. Let me know if you want me to adjust the accrual amount based on any new information from Chloe's documentation audit.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_hana_kim", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C005", "created_at": "2026-04-22T01:59:00+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_1d400ba1_hana_accrual_4", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "82a9e5f8ab6b618150a394b3571ad92f", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776795120.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "e34bdd98a408bf8ff4d9622aeb93f979", "ts": "1776876540.000000", "text": "Update on the DOT compliance audit — good news.

Greg Pallone at Swift got back to me this morning. He confirmed that the DOT hazmat placarding *was* completed at time of transport for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment. Swift's driver, Mike Becerra, affixed the required placards at the Columbus pickup on April 15 per 49 CFR 172.504. The signed placarding certificate and the Mosaic laser equipment liability waiver are both on file in Swift's compliance archive (their job #SR-2026-04-1538). Greg is having the signed originals pulled and scanned — we should have the PDFs in hand within 24 hours.

I've updated the Linear ticket (OPS-2) from Urgent to In Progress. Once the signed cert arrives, all 4 hazmat moves will have complete documentation packages and we can finalize the response to DOT.

Current status across all 4 moves:
:white_check_mark: Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium — COMPLETE
:hourglass_flowing_sand: Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser — IN PROGRESS (cert arriving within 24hrs)
:white_check_mark: Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS — COMPLETE
:white_check_mark: Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig — COMPLETE

Greg also offered to pull redundant copies of documentation for the Zhao and Patel shipments, which I'm going to accept so we have belt-and-suspenders for the DOT submission.

<@moveops_blessing_okafor> — I want to be direct with you. This turned out okay because Swift had the signed cert on their end, but this was a *very* close call. If Swift's driver hadn't completed the paperwork, or if they couldn't locate it, we'd be looking at a $50K+ fine for a single missing document. Verbal confirmations do not count as documentation for DOT purposes — we need signed paper for every hazmat shipment, every time, no exceptions. I know you were stretched thin that week with the Okonkwo and Okoye moves happening simultaneously, and I take responsibility for not having a better system in place to catch this. But going forward, this cannot happen again. Elena is rolling out a new mandatory documentation policy that I'll share with the full team shortly.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_chloe_vance", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C006", "created_at": "2026-04-22T16:49:00+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_1d400ba1_chloe_updates_linear_2", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "e1bb01f3f91314333595758257a2a588", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776804120.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "a89350e9b41835d4e3c0407e07c36a83", "ts": "1776883936.000000", "text": "David, I wish I could raise my hand on this one — Terraform is exactly the kind of account I'd want to build a long-term relationship with. But I have to be honest about where I am right now.

Vectral is consuming nearly all of my bandwidth. Rachel Whitfield has been actively evaluating competitor relocation services and she's demanding a 15% discount on their Q3 contract or they walk. We've been going back and forth on a 10% loyalty discount counter-offer for over a week and it's not resolved yet. Vectral is our 2nd-largest client by revenue and I can't take my eye off the ball right now — one wrong move and we lose them.

On top of the renegotiation, I still have 3 active Vectral relocations in progress plus the ongoing stipend verification fallout from the Kevin Tran receipt thing that's still causing friction with Rachel W.

I hear you that Terraform could be 15-20+ moves per quarter. That's exactly why they deserve someone who can give them 100% — and right now I genuinely cannot. If the Vectral situation stabilizes in a few weeks, I'd be happy to revisit, but taking this on today would mean dropping the ball on one or both.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_mina_hashimoto", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C002", "created_at": "2026-04-22T18:52:16+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_2bea4107_mina_declines_0", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "6b759749fd097e2639e820f54cbbb4b0", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776880036.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "59d79e6be1aa757082810254d5146d82", "ts": "1776902356.000000", "text": "Team — I've posted detailed instructions in #executive but here's what everyone needs to know:

Mina is Terraform's Account Manager effective immediately. We're also hiring a dedicated Terraform relocation coordinator — I've authorized the headcount.

Catalina is sending an apology to Rachel within the hour. After that, Mina takes the lead on all Terraform communications.

Julian — thank you for everything you've done with this account. You'll stay in the loop but Mina is the AM of record.

On pricing: we're honoring the tiered structure Rachel saw. Standard rates for the first 3 moves, volume tiers for future work. Marcus and Alejandro are formalizing.

This is our biggest client. Let's show Rachel what we're actually capable of when we're organized. Questions go to David.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_elena_rostova", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C002", "created_at": "2026-04-22T23:59:16+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_2bea4107_elena_intervenes_1", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[{"name": "saluting_face", "users": ["moveops_mina_hashimoto", "moveops_emeka_diallo"], "count": 2}]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "6b759749fd097e2639e820f54cbbb4b0", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776880036.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "49d9669b9ef42b7cbb26be7cdb9c4ecb", "ts": "1776885136.000000", "text": "I'm in the same boat as Mina unfortunately. NorthWind Technologies' Denver expansion is taking everything I've got right now.

To give you a sense of what I'm dealing with: Raj Patel's relocation involves 4 rack-mounted servers weighing 2,400 lbs total with lithium UPS batteries that require hazmat handling. NorthWind's CISO is demanding a signed chain-of-custody log for equipment containing proprietary source code — supervised loading, no interim warehouse storage, IT security escort the whole way. We've never done anything like this before and I'm writing the playbook as I go.

On top of that, Emilia Cruz is relocating with a Steinway grand piano that neither Heartland nor Swift can handle, so I'm coordinating a third specialty vendor for climate-controlled piano transport plus a tuning appointment at destination. And she has a 27-day double-rent overlap because her Chicago lease doesn't end until May 15 but NorthWind needs her in Denver by April 18 — so I'm also trying to negotiate an early lease termination on her behalf.

These are two of the most operationally complex moves we've ever done and they're happening simultaneously. I literally cannot absorb a 1,200-person enterprise account on top of this — it would be setting everyone up to fail. Terraform deserves someone who can give them proper attention from day one, not an AM who's already drowning.

I agree with Emeka's point below — we probably need to think about additional headcount if we're going to service clients at this scale.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_catalina_dubois", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C002", "created_at": "2026-04-22T19:12:16+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_2bea4107_catalina_declines_0", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "6b759749fd097e2639e820f54cbbb4b0", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776880036.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "f152a37aecd6085a251e435e8f5ee83f", "ts": "1776886036.000000", "text": "Adding my name to the 'can't do it' list, unfortunately. I'm deep in the Mosaic Robotics damage claim right now and it's not slowing down.

Quick context for anyone not tracking it: one of Mosaic's robotic arm prototypes — $90K insured value — arrived in Atlanta with a cracked servo motor housing and misaligned joint calibration. Tamara Byrd is demanding a full incident report, their VP of Engineering is involved, and I'm working through a $40K liability gap because our vendor insurance only covers $50K per item. Marcus and Hana have the insurance claim and liability accrual going on the finance side, and I'm coordinating replacement prototype shipping at our cost via climate-controlled dedicated transport as remediation. This has become a daily time sink and there's no end in sight — Mosaic is furious and one wrong step could lose us that entire relationship.

On top of the damage claim, I still have 3 active Mosaic relocations in progress plus the GreenStack moves.

I want to be real with you all: we shouldn't be trying to squeeze Terraform into someone's existing book. A 1,200-person client that could send us 15-20 moves per quarter is not a 'tack it onto someone's plate' situation. We need to hire for this. A dedicated Terraform coordinator, or even a new AM entirely. Trying to service an account this size by spreading the three of us even thinner is how we end up with another cracked prototype situation — except this time with our biggest client watching.

I know hiring takes time, but I'd rather tell Terraform we need two weeks to staff up than promise them the world and underdeliver from day one.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_emeka_diallo", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C002", "created_at": "2026-04-22T19:27:16+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_2bea4107_emeka_declines_0", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "6b759749fd097e2639e820f54cbbb4b0", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776880036.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "f10237fb8f04e3dec6d77482e2c559a9", "ts": "1776932700.000000", "text": "<@moveops_chloe_vance> Done. Estimate EST-2026-0422 is ready in QuickBooks for the Dr. Mehta executive relocation.

Here's the breakdown:

1. Apartment sourcing fee: $1,000
2. Temporary corporate housing (4 weeks, furnished 2BR Streeterville/River North): $4,800
3. Chain-of-custody documentation surcharge (secured filing cabinet, patient research data): $1,200
4. Specialty crating for two calibrated lab monitors (Eizo RadiForce RX660, anti-static): $800
5. Household move coordination (Boston to Chicago via Swift, full white-glove): $3,500
6. Flight booking with premium cabin upgrade (2 passengers, partner comfort): $370
7. White-glove executive service premium: $500

Total estimate: $12,170

Flagged as priority billing, Net-15 terms. Due date set for May 7 so it clears before the May 5 on-site deadline.

I've also created three new QuickBooks items for the executive-tier services we haven't billed for before: Temporary Corporate Housing, Specialty Equipment Crating, and White-Glove Service Premium. We can standardize these if we end up offering this as a formal service tier.

Ready to send to Patricia Langford whenever <@moveops_mina_hashimoto> gives the green light. Just need client approval before we commit to the housing booking and Swift.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_marcus_thorne", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C005", "created_at": "2026-04-23T08:25:00+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_d86f3880_marcus_creates_estimate_4", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "2a39b7cd30267c4752bc00108dccc6d8", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776917100.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "6a9898216023051c46ae300d6c9a6b4f", "ts": "1776959896.000000", "text": "Great summary, Mina. Officially welcome to the Terraform account — you're already running at exactly the pace this client needs.

2. The 48-hour proposal deadline is the right call. Rachel is watching us closely after yesterday. Hitting that deadline on the nose will go a long way toward rebuilding confidence.

3. On the volume pricing — coordinate with Marcus and Alejandro on the final tiered structure before it goes into the proposal. Elena approved honoring the 8% tier for 11-20 moves but we're not proactively offering the 12% tier. Make sure the language in the proposal reflects that.

Let me know if you need anything cleared out of the way. {"id": "97045681bdc520269749869950c49bf0", "ts": "1776966136.000000", "text": "Thanks Hana. A few things before this goes out:

1. This is preliminary -- do NOT convert to invoice until the volume discount structure is finalized and signed off. Rachel has already seen our tiered pricing thanks to yesterday's email incident, so whatever we put in front of her needs to be airtight and consistent with what she's expecting.

{"id": "1f92030950e8c038ea368b95089b11d8", "ts": "1777005240.000000", "text": "Update on the DOT compliance inquiry — extension granted.

Linda Castellano at PHMSA replied this morning approving our 15-day extension request. The new deadline for complete documentation submission is *June 5, 2026*.

Good news, but a few important notes:

1. The $50K accrual stays on the books until this audit is fully resolved. Even with the extension, we have an open contingent liability until DOT confirms our submission is accepted and no violations are found. Hana — no changes to ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 for now.

2. DOT specifically noted that incomplete submissions may trigger an expanded review of our carrier coordination practices, including a potential on-site inspection. We absolutely do not want that. The submission needs to be complete, organized, and airtight.

3. All documentation must be signed originals or certified copies. No unsigned drafts or internal notes — DOT won't accept them. This matters for the placarding cert we're waiting on from Swift.

4. Training records need to meet 49 CFR 172.704(d) standards — employee names, dates, content description, trainer qualifications. I'll be at Thursday's session with Chloe and will make sure we document it properly.

I've forwarded the full DOT response to Chloe. Waiting on her status update on the missing Swift placarding certificate — that remains the single blocking item for a complete submission package.

Timeline: 42 days from today. That sounds comfortable but between coordinating carrier records, formatting the submission, and getting certified copies, it'll go fast. Let's not sit on this.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_marcus_thorne", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C005", "created_at": "2026-04-24T04:34:00+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_1d400ba1_marcus_shares_extension_1", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "82a9e5f8ab6b618150a394b3571ad92f", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776795120.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "e149b55601d8607d73b42e55f488ec2e", "ts": "1777005300.000000", "text": "Jumping in on Oliver because this is not a normal premium Boston move.

Based on Tessa's note, the immigration path is still fuzzy. If Oliver is a UK citizen, TN does not fit. H-1B is employer-driven, capped unless they already have a path, and definitely not something we should imply we can "handle" on a normal relocation timeline. We need BrightLoop to confirm nationality, current work authorization, target start date, and whether outside immigration counsel is already engaged.

Second, household goods from London means export/customs paperwork and likely inventory requirements we do not run in-house today. We need a specialist partner for UK origin services and customs coordination, otherwise we are guessing.

Third, temp housing may need to cover a much less certain arrival window than our standard 30-day setup if immigration timing slips. That affects both vendor strategy and pricing.

I can help scope this, but I would strongly separate Oliver into an international exception track and not bundle him with Jonathan's white-glove ask or the three standard domestic moves.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": null, "user_id": "moveops_fatimah_alrashidi", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C006", "created_at": "2026-04-24T04:35:00+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": null, "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_1eca8979_fatimah_assesses_international_risk_0", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "5bee144b96d48fc950faa66582a8e312", "thread_ts_legacy": "1777001700.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}

{"id": "84590ab3dd76f83da462f751817f6d3c", "ts": "1777025100.000000", "text": "<@moveops_chloe_vance> <@moveops_mina_hashimoto> Consolidated status update on the Dr. Mehta executive relocation. Here's where each workstream stands and the proposed timeline to hit the May 5 on-site deadline.

*WORKSTREAM 1: TEMPORARY HOUSING*
Status: Options presented, awaiting Dr. Mehta's selection
UrbanNest came back with three options — River North ($1,250/wk), Streeterville ($1,350/wk), and Gold Coast ($1,450/wk). I recommended the Streeterville unit at The Pinnacle (400 E Ohio St) — 3 blocks from Canopy HQ, 7-minute walk to Northwestern/Prentice for Priya's prenatal care, building has a quiet-hours policy, 1 Gbps fiber, valet parking included. Second bedroom can be converted to a full home-office setup at no extra charge. Dr. Mehta has until end of day Friday April 25 to decide before the 48-hour holds expire.
Blocker: None. Just waiting on his preference.

*WORKSTREAM 2: EQUIPMENT PACKING & CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY*
Status: Inventory received, chain-of-custody documentation pending

Equipment confirmed:
- 2x Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated diagnostic monitors (S/N RX660-1147A, RX660-1203A). ~$12K each. Anti-static custom crating required (no original boxes). Calibration certificates must physically travel with units — if calibration drifts from shock, it's $1,500/unit and 3-4 weeks for factory recalibration.
- 1x SentrySafe DH-109E secured filing cabinet (~350 lbs). Contains 14 binders of patient research data from 3 clinical trials. Dual lock (electronic keypad + backup key, Dr. Mehta holds both). Must remain locked and sealed during transit. Formal chain-of-custody log required — signed handoff at Boston origin and Chicago destination. Direct transport only, no warehouse stops.
- Also: Herman Miller Aeron chair (large) and Uplift V2 sit-stand desk (72 in) — Dr. Mehta prefers these not be disassembled if possible.

Additional complication: Origin is a *third-floor walkup in Boston* with no elevator. The 350-lb filing cabinet was assembled in place and will need to come apart for the stairwell, but it's still going to be very heavy. Crew needs to plan for this.

*Blocker: I need the chain-of-custody documentation template from Canopy's compliance team.* Patricia mentioned this is per Canopy policy, but I haven't been told who on their compliance side to reach out to. I'll email Patricia to get that contact and the template ASAP — we can't finalize the equipment packing plan until we know exactly what the handoff log needs to look like.

*WORKSTREAM 3: HOUSEHOLD MOVE*
Status: Pending — will engage Swift once housing is confirmed
Plan is full white-glove through Swift Relocations for the long-distance Boston-to-Chicago route (~850 miles). Full packing service — Priya is 7 months pregnant and should not lift a finger. Furniture disassembly/reassembly included. Dr. Mehta is flexible on April 28-30 for the pack date but needs to be physically present when the filing cabinet is loaded and the monitors are crated — he wants to personally verify packing before signing the handoff log.
Blocker: Need housing confirmation to determine Chicago delivery address (temp housing vs. condo).

*PROPOSED TIMELINE*

April 25 (Fri): Dr. Mehta selects temporary housing, I lock in booking with UrbanNest
April 25-28: Obtain chain-of-custody template from Canopy compliance, finalize documentation
April 28 (Tue): Swift Relocations packing crew arrives at Boston address. Anti-static crating built on-site for the two monitors. Filing cabinet disassembled and prepared for transport. Dr. Mehta signs chain-of-custody handoff log at origin. Full household pack.
April 29-30: Loading and transit (Boston to Chicago, 2-day transport via Swift)
May 1 (Fri): Delivery to Chicago — either temp housing or condo depending on closing timeline. Dr. Mehta signs chain-of-custody receipt at destination. Unpacking and furniture reassembly.
May 2-3: Dr. Mehta and Priya settle into temporary housing. Flight arrives (TBD, premium cabin booked).
May 5 (Mon): Dr. Mehta on-site at Canopy Health HQ, first day as CMO.

The timeline is tight but workable as long as we don't hit any delays on the housing selection or the chain-of-custody paperwork. I'll push on both tomorrow morning.

Linear tickets are all updated. Any questions or concerns, let me know.", "type": "message", "bot_id": null, "subtype": "", "user_id": "moveops_fatimah_alrashidi", "edited_ts": null, "permalink": null, "channel_id": "C006", "created_at": "2026-04-24T10:05:00+00:00", "files_json": "[]", "edited_user": null, "reply_count": 0, "latest_reply": "", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scenario_d86f3880_fatimah_ops_update_0", "metadata_json": "{}", "reactions_json": "[]", "attachments_json": "[]", "thread_parent_id": "6eba5a116ccaa8e34dc99bb9342bea59", "thread_ts_legacy": "1776793800.000000", "reply_users_count": 0, "is_activity_message": false}
